_protocol: Calibration topic — Strait of Hormuz closure duration forecast. Original flagship topic, tracking since 2026-03-02. 124 evidence entries. Ceasefire agreed Apr 8, collapsed Apr 11. Islamabad talks failed. Trump announced US naval blockade Apr 12. Now dual blockade (Iran + US). Mine-clearing ops underway.

meta.title: HORMUZ STRAIT CLOSURE
meta.question: How long will the Strait of Hormuz remain effectively closed?
meta.resolution: Sustained >30% of pre-war Hormuz traffic (~40 transits/day)

posteriorHistory.redTeamSummary: 0.7554 on H3 still strong: Navy 6mo clearance anchors mid-H3, dual blockade ongoing, mining continues. H4 0.2346 may be high - every indicator's max LR was H4=1.0 by operator design choice creating systematic H4 over-attribution. H2 0.005 may be low - Iran's storage exhaustion (~13 days) is real economic forcing toward near-term reopening not yet captured by anti-indicators. If iran_storage_exhaustion or iran_reopen_proposal fire prospectively H2 recovers. 0.7554 defensible as indicator-chain result.

khargTimeline.assaultWindow: ACTIVE — initial ops underway as of Apr 7
khargTimeline.note: EVOLVED SIGNIFICANTLY: Tripoli offshore Kharg running F-35B SEAD missions. SEAL/Raider 'Quiet Entry' ops on southern tip reported. 50+ military targets struck Apr 7. IRGC claims missile hit on Tripoli (unconfirmed). Boxer ARG arrived in theater. 50,000+ US troops now in region. Trump gave 'Execute' order for initial phase per some reports. Simultaneously, mine-clearing ops launched in Hormuz (USS Peterson + USS Murphy + UUVs). Mission is now multi-track: Kharg seizure + Hormuz clearing + blockade enforcement.

trumpUltimatum.note: RESOLVED: Deadline extended 3x (Mar 23→28→Apr 6→Apr 7), then suspended for 2-week ceasefire (Apr 8). Ceasefire collapsed after 21hr Islamabad talks (Apr 11). Trump pivoted to naval blockade (Apr 12) + Kharg military strikes (Apr 7) + mine clearing (Apr 11). Outcome = hybrid of D (extended into ceasefire) then C (pivoted to ground/maritime ops after talks failed). Power plants never hit. Submodel effectively resolved — Trump's ultimatum cycle ended with blockade announcement.

direct_talks_resume.label: Direct talks resume after cooling period

maximalist_deadlock.label: Both sides' positions too far apart, talks collapse

talksTrack.note: RESOLVED PHASE 1: Indirect relay channel escalated to face-to-face talks in Islamabad (Apr 11). Vance/Witkoff/Kushner met Iranian delegation — first since 2015. 21hr marathon. COLLAPSED. Irreconcilable sticking points: (1) Iran nuclear enrichment — US demands halt, Iran refuses, (2) Hormuz tolls — US demands free passage, Iran claims sovereignty right, (3) Lebanon — Iran demands Israel stop Hezbollah strikes, US says excluded. Trump pivoted to blockade within hours of collapse. Channel exists (Pakistan mediation) but positions too far apart for near-term deal. Direct talks now 0.30 — Islamabad established the precedent of face-to-face, parties could return after pressure cycle. But maximalist_deadlock dominant at 0.55.

tier2_strong.desc: Named DoD/Navy official or Congressional testimony stating mine-clearance operations require >=6 months from commencement of clearing. Captures the post-conflict clearance tail as a duration component separable from conflict duration. Observable threshold: publicly reported testimony/briefing from a named official, corroborated by at least one independent outlet.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H3->H4 mass shift (~5-10pp from H3, 3-5pp from H2/H1 residual to H4). Reflects that if clearance alone takes >=26 weeks and can only begin after hostilities cease, any remaining-conflict duration >0 pushes total toward H4. Apply once per distinct testimony event; do not re-fire on restatements of the same underlying DoD estimate.
tier2_strong.design_note: Added 2026-04-24 to close indicator-tier gap: clearance-duration testimony was previously uncategorized and being logged as impact=NONE (see ev_173). Closes blind spot flagged by governance alert on this topic.
tier2_strong.note: FIRED via canvas action button. Testimony that Hormuz mine clearance requires ~6 months captures post-conflict clearance tail as a duration component independent of conflict duration. Evidence: ev_173 (US Navy officials to Congress, Apr 2026).

actorModel.description: Trump-adjusted decision framework with four layers

trump.name: President Trump
trump.decisionStyle: Impulsive, fixation-driven, spectacle-optimizing. Evaluates operations on immediate visual results, not long-term strategic cost.
biases: Claims credit for things not happening (fake 'talks')
filters: Military planning process filters novel stupidity
overrides: Personal fixations bypass tactical filter â€” Kharg qualifies because assault will succeed on Day 1

centcom.name: CENTCOM / Joint Staff
centcom.decisionStyle: Competent tactical execution within parameters given. 9,000+ combat flights, 9,000+ targets hit.
filters: Normal planning process filters tactically idiotic ideas
overrides: Direct presidential order gets executed regardless of strategic risk assessment

iran.name: Iranian leadership (IRGC + civilian)
iran.role: Adversary â€” controls Hormuz closure and escalation pace
iran.decisionStyle: Attrition-oriented, escalatory after nuclear site strikes. NOW: blocked Chinese ships (corridor dead), IRGC warned civilians to evacuate near US bases (pre-attack signal), vows retaliation 'no longer eye for eye.' IRGC consolidating power: Zolghadr at SNSC, Tangsiri dead with no replacement = C2 void in IRGC Navy. Mojtaba Khamenei still not seen. 2/3 of missiles intact â€” can sustain attacks for months.
overrides: Counter-threat on power plants appears to have deterred Trump â€” first successful Iranian deterrence in this conflict

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC â€” only kinetic events, force movements, budget requests, verified data move posteriors. Trump words discounted. IRGC threats tagged THREATENED until executed.
SOCIALIZATION PATTERN RECOGNITION â€” 'agonizing decision' articles with hyper-specific operational detail = narrative prep for decision already on conveyor belt
DON'T FRONT-RUN â€” set tripwires and let news confirm. Posteriors move when indicators fire, not when thesis feels good.
TRUMP FIXATION OVERRIDE â€” when CinC has personal fixation predating the situation, tactical filter is bypassed. Kharg = 38yr fixation.
TRUMP RHETORIC DISCOUNT â€” 'talks' claim assessed as face-saving for ultimatum blink. Iran denied. Mediator relay confirmed but is not negotiation. DO NOT move posteriors on Trump words alone.

evidenceLog.text: Iran fires 8th missile salvo of the day at Israel. IRGC targeting Arad, Dimona, Eilat, Beersheba, Kiryat Gat. 100+ wounded. Sustained inventory depth.
Israel strikes Qasmiya Bridge over Litani River. DM Katz ordered destruction of ALL Litani crossings. Lebanese President Aoun: 'prelude to ground invasion.'
Iran Parliament Speaker Qalibaf: 'Critical infrastructure, energy infrastructure, and oil facilities throughout the region will be destroyed in irreversible manner' if power plants hit.
Trump: 'weeks ahead of schedule,' Iran's 'navy and air force are dead, they have absolutely no defense.' Victory framing â€” ambiguous whether prelude to declare-victory or Kharg push.
Brent Sunday open $106.41, range $105-113. Elevated but no panic gap. Bitcoin dropped to $69,200 on ultimatum, recovered to $70,800.
Tripoli ARG arrival window open. No confirmed theater arrival yet as of 1439 PST. Still the single most important near-term indicator.

Trump postponed power plant strikes 5 days. Claimed 'very good and productive conversations' with Iran that could yield 'complete and total resolution.' Classic Trump face-saving pivot â€” ultimatumâ†’blinkâ†’claim diplomacy.

Iran Foreign Ministry: 'There is no dialogue between Tehran and Washington.' BUT CBS exclusive: senior Iranian FM official confirmed 'received points from US through mediators, being reviewed.' Mediator relay â‰  negotiations.
evidenceLog.source: CBS News exclusive / Al Jazeera / NPR

evidenceLog.text: Iran Operation True Promise IV: struck Israel (Tel Aviv, Haifa) + Gulf states (Kuwait airport/power lines, Bahrain fuel tanks, UAE Abu Dhabi, Saudi Riyadh/Eastern Province, Jordan nationwide sirens, Iraq Erbil). War regionalized beyond Israel-Iran axis.

Iran letter to IMO: 'non-hostile' ships may transit Hormuz in coordination with Iranian authorities. US/Israel/coalition vessels explicitly excluded. Blockade formalized as international legal framework. First Chinese cargo ship (Newvoyager) used Iran's Larak-Qeshm corridor Mar 23.

82nd Airborne deploying ~1,000 troops to Middle East including division commander Maj Gen Tegtmeier and 1st BCT battalion (Immediate Response Force). Ground war preparation. Pentagon expected to confirm.
USS Tripoli in port Diego Garcia as of Mar 23. NOT yet in Gulf of Oman/Persian Gulf. Pushed Kharg assault window right ~2 days. ETA Gulf of Oman ~Mar 27-28.
Brent crashed 11% Monday on 'talks' news to ~$91, recovered Tuesday to $103-104. WTI ~$91. Gas national avg $3.97/gal (up $1.02 in March). Market whipsaw â€” skepticism over de-escalation.
~3,200 vessels stranded near Hormuz. ~200 non-sanctioned tankers stuck in Gulf. 21 total tanker transits since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. Iran corridor seeing first traffic (Chinese ship).
Casualties: US KIA 14, ~200 wounded (most returned to duty). Iran dead ~5,900 including 595 civilians per Hengaw 21-day report through Mar 20. Lebanon 880 dead, Iraq 61 dead.

Senator urged Kharg attack referencing 'bloodiest battle in USMC history' (Military.com Mar 23). Bloomberg opinion: 'Kharg is big risk for little reward.' Congressional debate = socialization continuing.

US sent Iran 15-point plan via Pakistan. Reported terms: dismantle nuclear program (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan), halt enrichment, hand enriched materials to IAEA, stop financing proxies, declare Hormuz free maritime zone. In exchange: sanctions relief, civilian nuclear support at Bushehr. Israel Channel 12 leaked outline.

Trump claims Vance, Rubio, Witkoff, Kushner involved in talks. Claims Iran offered 'very big present' related to 'oil and gas.' Declared war 'won.' Iran IRGC and parliament call this 'fake news' and 'big lie.' Pattern: victory framing + talk fabrication for face-saving.

Iranian source told CNN Tehran 'willing to listen to sustainable proposals to end the war.' First signal beyond flat denial. Pakistan FM offered to host US-Iran talks. Mediator relay track continuing. Still not negotiations but relay channel alive.

82nd Airborne deployment upgraded: ~2,000 troops (two battalions + enabling units), up from initial ~1,000 reports. Verbal orders for 1st BCT + second battalion. Div commander + full division staff. Larger ground force posture than initially assessed.
Container ship attacked 35nm north of Jebel Ali (UAE) per British Maritime Trade Operations. Iran blockade enforcement extending beyond Hormuz strait into wider Gulf.

Iran corridor traffic growing: fees up to $2M/transit. Japan now allowed through alongside China/India. 11.7M barrels crude to China since war began. Blockade becoming selective commercial regime. Western/coalition ships still excluded.
Brent recovered to $104.49/bbl Tue after crashing to ~$99 Mon on 'talks' news. Market skeptical of de-escalation. 82,000+ civilian structures damaged in Iran per Red Crescent.

Posteriors HELD: No new indicators fired. 15-point plan is rhetoric-grade until Iran responds with action. Trump talk claims contradicted by Iran. 82nd upgrade material but indicator already fired. Iran 'willing to listen' is first positive signal but not actionable yet.

David's Sling interceptor failure: 2 Iranian Qader-family ballistic missiles struck Dimona and Arad (southern Israel) on Mar 22. IDF chose David's Sling over Arrow to conserve Arrow stocks. ~200 wounded, major damage. Dimona widely believed to house Israel's nuclear arsenal. IDF says not systemic failure.
Iranian missiles hit Tel Aviv Mar 24, major building damage, at least 4 casualties. Continued sustained missile inventory depth despite 25 days of war.

Saudi Arabia intercepted ~20 drones targeting Eastern Province (critical oil/energy region) on Mon Mar 24. Kuwait alarms sounded 7 times in one night. Bahrain: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted since war start. Gulf states under sustained Iranian fire.

Lebanon declared Iran ambassador-designate Shibani persona non grata Mar 24, gave until Mar 29 to leave. Cited IRGC directing Hezbollah. Lebanon recalled its envoy to Iran. Hezbollah condemned as 'sin,' demanded reversal. First Iranian missile intercepted over Lebanese airspace.

Iran appointed IRGC veteran Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as new SNSC secretary, replacing Ali Larijani (killed Mar 17). Former IRGC joint staff head (8yr) and deputy IRGC commander (8yr). Consolidates IRGC control of security apparatus. Decision-making chain hardening.

Islamabad peace summit proposed for as early as Thu Mar 27. US reps: Kushner, Witkoff, possibly Vance. Iran delegation led by Qalibaf. Mediators: Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey. BUT: Mojtaba Khamenei must approve and is injured/isolated/not responding to messages per US/Israeli intel. Iran has NOT agreed to attend.

Hormuz corridor expanding: 6 vessels transited with AIS on Mar 24 (up from 3 on Mar 23). 16 AIS-visible crossings in past 7 days. Iran corridor fees up to $2M/transit. China, India, Japan, Pakistan approved. ~3,200 vessels still stranded. Traffic still 95%+ below pre-war.

USS Tripoli confirmed at Diego Garcia pier Mar 23 (satellite imagery). WSJ: Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) expected to cross into CENTCOM AOR Friday Mar 28. Several additional days to reach Hormuz after that. Kharg assault window pushed to ~Apr 1-5.
US airstrike in Anbar province (Iraq) targeted Iran-backed group HQ, senior commander Saad Dawai targeted. War expanding into Iraq theater.

Brent fell to $98.57 (prev close $95.92, range $96-100). Down from $104.49 â€” market partially pricing de-escalation on 'talks' narrative. WTI $90.98 (range $88.54-92.68). Gas national avg $3.956. Oil still elevated but below recent highs.

Philippines declared national energy emergency (EO 110) Mar 24 â€” 95-98% of oil imports via Middle East, 2-month supply remaining. South Korea PM canceled China trip over economic fallout (70%+ oil from ME). ADNOC chief called blockade 'economic terrorism.' International pressure mounting.

Trump approval hit 36% (Reuters/Ipsos poll, completed Mon Mar 24) â€” record low. Only 25% approve cost-of-living handling. Only 35% approve Iran strikes (down from 37%). 61% disapprove of strikes. Domestic political pressure building against escalation.

Iran damage: 82,000+ civilian structures damaged/destroyed (Red Crescent). 466 arrested for alleged online destabilization. Iran dead ~1,444 confirmed including 204 children (Al Jazeera tracker). Lebanon: 1,001 dead, 2,584 wounded, 1M+ displaced. US KIA: 13 confirmed (7 enemy fire, 6 non-hostile/crash).

Mojtaba Khamenei status: fractured foot, face lacerations, 'wounded and likely disfigured' per Hegseth. Not seen/heard since appointment. US/Israeli intel: injured, isolated, not responding to messages. Must approve Islamabad summit attendance. Decision-making paralysis at top of Iranian system possible.

Posteriors HELD: No new tier 1/2 indicators fired. Key dynamics: (1) corridor expanding but blockade still 95%+ effective, (2) Islamabad summit proposed but Iran hasn't agreed and supreme leader incapacitated, (3) Tripoli ETA pushed to Mar 28 CENTCOM AOR then +days to Hormuz, (4) oil markets softening on talks narrative but skeptical, (5) IRGC consolidating control via Zolghadr appointment â€” hardliner replacing pragmatist signals no near-term concessions. Net: blockade institutionalizing, ground operation timeline extending, diplomatic track uncertain. Hold.

CONFIRMED: ~12 Iranian naval mines deployed in Strait of Hormuz (CBS, US officials). Two types: Maham 3 (moored, magnetic/acoustic sensors) and Maham 7 (seabed limpet, acoustic/3-axis magnetic). Deployed by small vessels carrying 2-3 mines each. Iran total mine inventory estimated 2,000-6,000. Mines physically extend closure timeline regardless of diplomacy â€” clearance takes weeks/months.
evidenceLog.source: CBS News exclusive, US intelligence officials

evidenceLog.text: Iran CATEGORICALLY REJECTED 15-point plan. IRGC Khatam al-Anbiya spokesperson Zolfaghari: 'Our first and last word has been the same from day one â€” someone like us will never come to terms with someone like you. Not now, not ever.' Iran mocks: 'Has the level of your internal conflict reached the point where you are negotiating with yourselves?' Flat rejection, not posturing.

Iran Defense Council warned: ANY attack on Iran's coasts or islands will trigger mining of ALL access routes and sea lanes in the Persian Gulf. Direct counter-threat to Kharg operation. Would extend blockade from Hormuz to entire Gulf. Iran has 2,000-6,000 mine inventory.

Bushehr NPP hit by projectile SECOND TIME (first was Mar 17). IAEA informed by Iran â€” no damage to plant or injuries. IAEA DG Grossi reiterates 'maximum restraint.' Bushehr strikes accelerating â€” partial indicator upgrading.
evidenceLog.source: World Nuclear News/CGTN/IAEA official X post

evidenceLog.text: Day 26 strikes: Southern Tehran raid killed 12, wounded 28. Eastern Tehran school + residential buildings destroyed. Kuwait airport fuel tank hit by drone (fire, no casualties). Saudi shot down 32 drones + 1 ballistic missile in Eastern Province over 11 hours. Bahrain: 1 Moroccan civilian killed.

82nd Airborne deployment upgraded AGAIN to ~3,000 soldiers (up from ~2,000, originally ~1,000). Combined with 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) = ~5,200 ground troops heading to theater. Force-sizing consistent with Kharg operation.

Iraq: 15 killed in suspected US strike on paramilitary base. Iraqi government granted Iran-backed groups RIGHT TO RETALIATE against US forces. Islamic Resistance Iraq claims 23 operations against US bases in 24 hours. Iraq theater actively widening.

Lebanon casualties updated: 1,072 killed, 2,966 wounded since Mar 2 (33 deaths in last 24h). Israel announces 30km 'security zone' into Lebanon south of Litani River. Hezbollah attacking Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon and Golan Heights.

Qalibaf: 'We are closely monitoring all US movements in the region, especially troop deployments.' Warns against 'sacrificing American soldiers for Israeli interests.' Iran interpreting troop deployments as proof talks are a ruse. Islamabad summit increasingly unlikely â€” Iran told mediators troop movements increase suspicion.

Israel says NOT participating in US-Iran talks. Military ops will continue until Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities eliminated. Israel fears premature ceasefire (Times of Israel). US-Israel divergence on endstate emerging.

US eased some Iranian oil sanctions due to global demand pressures (Al Jazeera Day 26). Brent ~$99.30-99.75 (down 5% intraday on talks optimism). Sri Lanka ordered streetlights/neon off to cut energy consumption 25%. Helium and fertilizer supplies from Gulf disrupted.

POSTERIORS UPDATED (minor). Mines in Hormuz strait (ACTION, not rhetoric) + Iran's categorical rejection of 15-point plan + mining threat for entire Gulf if Kharg attacked = blockade hardening physically and diplomatically. H2 (6wk-4mo) down 3pp to 0.16, absorbed by H3 (+1pp to 0.53) and H4 (+2pp to 0.30). Rationale: mines add weeks/months to clearance regardless of political outcome; Iran's rejection eliminates near-term diplomatic offramp; Kharg operation now triggers Gulf-wide mining. E[weeks] rises to 39.32.

CRITICAL: Iran issued 5-POINT COUNTER-PROPOSAL (not just rejection). Conditions: (1) halt aggression/assassinations by US+Israel, (2) mechanisms to ensure war doesn't resume, (3) payment of war damages/reparations, (4) end attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon and pro-Iran militias in Iraq, (5) INTERNATIONAL RECOGNITION AND GUARANTEES FOR IRAN'S AUTHORITY OVER STRAIT OF HORMUZ. Point 5 is a fundamental new demand â€” Iran seeking permanent sovereignty over Hormuz as a war objective.

Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire' â€” says ceasefire would lead to vicious cycle of repeated war. Wants war to end 'on our own terms.' Also: exchange of messages through mediators 'does not mean negotiations.' Called US proposal 'maximalist, unreasonable.' This is NOT a breakdown â€” it's a maximalist opening position. Iran is engaging through counter-proposal even while denying negotiations.

White House: Trump will 'unleash hell' if Iran doesn't accept defeat. Leavitt: 'Trump does not bluff.' Claims US 'ahead of schedule' on original 4-6 week timeline. Claims 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed including ~50 mine layers. Claims Iran attacks 'down 90%.' Framing war as nearly won while deploying 3,000 more troops.

UN Security Council: TWO draft resolutions on Hormuz. (1) Bahrain draft â€” Chapter VII, authorizes 'all necessary means' to open strait. 22 nations signal readiness including UK, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, Canada, South Korea. (2) Russia draft â€” shorter, doesn't name countries. Neither expected to vote this week. China/Russia likely veto Bahrain draft. UN chief: war 'totally out of control.'
evidenceLog.source: Security Council Report/Washington Times/Arab News/UN News

evidenceLog.text: White House claims: 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed including ~50 mine layers. 9,000+ enemy targets struck. Iran's attacks down ~90%. If mine layer destruction numbers accurate, degrades Iran's ability to deploy remaining 2,000-6,000 mine inventory â€” but ~12 already in strait still need clearing.

Brent continued falling â€” range $93.45-$102.22 depending on time of day (CNBC: $102.22, Investing.com: $96.68). Market whipsawing on talks narrative vs reality. WTI $90.32. Markets pricing de-escalation that isn't happening â€” disconnect between market narrative and ground truth widening.

Posteriors HELD this cycle. Iran's counter-proposal is MIXED signal: (1) demanding Hormuz sovereignty = intends permanent control (pushes H4), BUT (2) issuing counter-proposal at all = engaging in negotiation framework (pulls toward H2/H3). Net: offsetting. Counter-proposal tells us Iran sees Hormuz as permanent leverage, not temporary tactic. But 5-point framework means there IS a negotiating track â€” even if positions are miles apart. Key question: does US treat counter-proposal as basis for negotiation or as proof talks are dead? Watch White House response + whether Islamabad meeting materializes.

MAJOR: Israel killed IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri + IRGC Navy intelligence chief Behnam Rezaei in overnight strike on Bandar Abbas. Tangsiri was THE mastermind behind the Hormuz blockade. Katz: 'precise and lethal operation.' Targeted while meeting senior IRGC Navy commanders. Iran has not confirmed. System he built continues but decapitation of naval command is significant.

Iran fortifying Kharg Island: anti-personnel and anti-armor mines on shoreline/landing zones, MANPADs deployed, additional military personnel and air defenses moved in. US has persistent overhead surveillance tracking all changes. Iran 'laying traps' â€” they are 'clever, ruthless' per US officials. Makes Kharg seizure significantly more costly.

82nd Airborne deployment: WRITTEN orders issued (upgraded from verbal). 2,000-3,000 paratroopers. Combined with two MEUs = 6,000-8,000 US ground troops heading to Gulf. Pentagon preparing 'final blow' options including Kharg seizure and strait operations. Axios: Pentagon prepares for 'massive final blow.'

Iran blocked Pakistan-bound container ship SELEN (Sharjahâ†’Karachi, food supplies, St Kitts flag) at Hormuz â€” day AFTER Pakistan offered to mediate. IRGC cited lack of clearance. Signal: Iran's Hormuz control is non-negotiable, even against mediator countries. Blockade enforcement tightening, not loosening.

Israel completed 'wave of extensive strikes' in Isfahan targeting Iranian regime infrastructure. Israel also struck Shiraz, Bandar Abbas, Tabriz, Mashhad. Iran fired 2 rounds of missiles at central Israel causing destruction and injuries. IDF soldier killed in Lebanon gunfight. War tempo intensifying despite 'talks.'

GCC Secretary-General al-Budaiwi: Iran 'crossed all red lines.' Calls Hormuz closure and toll imposition 'aggression and violation of UNCLOS.' UN Human Rights Council resolution condemning Hormuz closure backed by 47 members. GCC says Iran's actions are 'turning point' in relationship. Iran legislating formal Hormuz toll system.

Trump: Iranian negotiators 'begging' for deal. White House: talks 'proceeding apace,' 'have not hit dead end.' Leavitt: US 'ahead of schedule' on 4-6 week timeline. Meanwhile Iran's Araghchi: 'We do not want a ceasefire.' Maximum disconnect between US victory framing and Iranian defiance. Both sides performing for domestic audiences while mediators shuttle.

Brent spiked to $105.85 (up $6.10 from previous morning) â€” market reversed de-escalation bet. Iran dead now 1,750+ (up from 1,444). Lebanon 1,094 dead, 2,966 wounded. Pew Research: 59% of Americans say US made wrong decision using force in Iran (59-38). Trump approval steady at record low.

Posteriors HELD â€” Tangsiri kill is hours old, operational impact unknown. Competing dynamics: (1) IRGC Navy decapitated â†’ could degrade blockade operations (shorter), BUT (2) Iran fortifying Kharg with mines/MANPADs (longer), (3) Iran blocking Pakistan ship = tightening not loosening (longer), (4) 'final blow' planning with written orders = military track accelerating, (5) Brent spiking = market pricing longer conflict. CRITICAL 48h ahead: Tripoli enters CENTCOM AOR Friday, ultimatum expires Friday, Tangsiri kill likely triggers Iranian escalation. All three converge on Mar 28. Hold and watch.

TRUMP EXTENDED ENERGY STRIKE PAUSE ANOTHER 10 DAYS TO APRIL 6 (8pm ET). Truth Social: 'As per Iranian Government request.' Claims talks 'going very well.' This is the SECOND extension (5 days Mar 23â†’28, now +10 to Apr 6). Pattern confirmed: ultimatumâ†’blinkâ†’claim diplomacyâ†’extend. Our Option D (extended/dropped) now 2x confirmed. Kharg window pushed right again â€” Apr 6+ at earliest.

Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar OFFICIALLY CONFIRMED 'indirect talks' â€” first mediator country to publicly confirm relay channel is real. 'US has shared 15 points, being deliberated upon by Iran.' Iran has RESPONDED through intermediaries, now AWAITING Washington's reply. Witkoff publicly discussed 15-point plan as 'framework for peace deal.' This is an actual negotiation dynamic â€” proposal, counter-proposal, relay â€” even if neither side calls it talks.

CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirmed Tangsiri kill, said Iran's navy on path to 'irreversible decline.' US hit 2/3 of Iran's missile/drone production facilities. Combined with 140+ naval vessels destroyed including ~50 mine layers. Iran's ability to sustain/expand blockade degrading â€” but existing mines and corridor system persist.

Brent closed at $108.01 (+5.66%). S&P 500 fell 1.74% â€” biggest daily drop of 2026. WTI $94.48 (+4.61%). Market REVERSED de-escalation bet. WTO chief: 'worst trade disruption in 80 years.' ~2,000 ships waiting on both sides of Hormuz.

Posteriors HELD. Trump's 10-day extension to Apr 6 is THE key development â€” pushes ALL military timelines right. Kharg window now Apr 6+ at earliest (was Apr 1-5). But: (1) indirect talks confirmed by Pakistan, (2) Iran responded through mediators & awaiting US reply = actual negotiation dynamic, (3) Tangsiri kill + naval degradation = Iran's blockade capability eroding, (4) BUT Iran fortifying Kharg and blocking even mediator country ships = hardening. Net: extension buys time for both diplomatic and military tracks. Hold â€” next decision point is Apr 6.

Trump reveals Iran allowed 10 oil tankers through Hormuz as 'very big present.' At Cabinet meeting: Iran said 'to show you we're real and solid, we'll let you have 8 boats of oil' â€” ended up being 10. Ships reportedly foreign-flagged (possibly Pakistani). Corridor traffic DID increase from 3â†’6â†’10 ships recently. But Iran simultaneously blocked Pakistan-bound SELEN. Selective passage â‰  reopening â€” Iran choosing who passes.

Iran formalizing yuan-denominated toll system for Hormuz transit. At least 2 vessels confirmed paying ~$2M/transit in yuan (Lloyd's List Intelligence). CIPS (China's payment system) transaction volume spiking. Iran parliament legislating formal toll structure. De facto sovereignty assertion over international waterway + petrodollar bypass. Mattis at CERAWeek: 'If we declared victory now, they'd own the strait and tax every ship.' That's already happening.

Treasury Sec Bessent: US considering releasing emergency crude reserves (SPR) and removing sanctions on in-transit Iranian crude to ease oil prices. Brent pulled back from $106.12 morning high on this news before closing at $108.01. SPR release = demand-side Band-Aid, not supply fix. Sanctions removal on in-transit crude = acknowledging Iran's corridor has economic value.

MAJOR: FM Araghchi FORMALLY announced 5-nation selective passage regime â€” China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan explicitly permitted through Hormuz. Malaysia separately confirmed allowed. Araghchi: 'We are in a state of war. The region is a war zone. No reason to allow ships of our enemies and their allies to pass through. But it remains open to others.' This transforms the de facto corridor into OFFICIAL IRANIAN FOREIGN POLICY. Creates stakeholders (China, Russia, India) who BENEFIT from the blockade status quo. Combined with yuan toll system, this is a sovereignty regime, not a crisis measure.

IDF mega-strike wave: 60 IAF jets dropped 150+ bombs on Parchin (Tehran) and Isfahan. Targeted 'most central facility for production of missiles and defense systems' â€” including warhead casting, engine mixing, solid-fuel ballistic missile components. Also struck IRGC Quds Force weapons site and air defense manufacturing in Isfahan. Industrial university in Isfahan targeted. Total: 15,000 Iranian targets hit since Feb 28. Daily target count dropped from initial ~1,000 to fewer than 100 â€” degradation campaign approaching diminishing returns.
evidenceLog.source: Times of Israel/JPost/YNet/Israel National News/Alma Center

evidenceLog.text: Iran fired TWO rounds of missiles at central Israel causing widespread damage. Impacts in Kafr Qasim and Ganei Tikva. 1 civilian killed in Nahariya by Hezbollah missile. Iran demonstrating sustained missile capability 27 days into war despite 2/3 of production facilities destroyed. Israeli defense system stress-tested â€” WaPo: 'Iran missiles pierce Israel's defenses, raising doubts about interceptors.'
evidenceLog.source: Al Jazeera/WaPo/Times of Israel liveblog Mar 26

evidenceLog.text: IDF Brig Gen Defrin: 'The eliminations will not stop; we will continue to pursue anyone who threatens Israel.' No replacement for Tangsiri announced by Iran. Tehran has NOT confirmed his death. IRGC Navy command structure decapitated â€” commander + intelligence chief + senior aides all killed. Institutional blockade system persists but C2 disrupted.

Hengaw 7th report: 6,530 killed in Iran in 25 days of war, including 640 civilians. Up from 5,900 in 21 days (6th report). Al Jazeera tracker (confirmed only): 1,750+. Lebanon: 1,072 killed, 2,966 wounded. Israel: 18 civilians killed, 5,229+ injured. US KIA: 13. Mojtaba Khamenei STILL not seen publicly â€” no new images/video since Mar 8 appointment. Incapacitation evidence mounting.
evidenceLog.source: Hengaw/Al Jazeera tracker/ACLED/Times of Israel/Critical Threats

evidenceLog.text: Gas national average $3.98/gal (AAA), up $1.00 in one month. Brent closed $108.01, WTI $94.48. S&P 500 fell 1.74% to lowest since September. Nasdaq entered correction territory (-2.4%). ECB President warned on inflation from oil shock. Oil market volatility surging â€” market reversed de-escalation bet. Polymers diverging from crude: oil correcting at times but polymer prices keep climbing (supply chain disruption outlasts price moves).

Posteriors HELD. Araghchi's 5-nation selective passage is the key evening development â€” it FORMALIZES the blockade into a sovereignty regime. But this is incremental institutionalization of trends already captured (IMO letter, corridor, toll system, 10 tankers). No new tier 1/2 indicators fired. IDF degradation campaign approaching diminishing returns (daily targets <100) while Iran STILL firing missiles = attrition equilibrium emerging. Tangsiri void significant but system persists. Net: blockade hardening into permanent structure while military campaign plateaus. Positions locked. Apr 6 remains next decision point.

CRITICAL: Two Chinese Cosco container ships (CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean) attempted to EXIT Persian Gulf through Hormuz, then made ABRUPT U-TURN near Larak/Qeshm islands and motored back. Bloomberg ship-tracking data. This happened HOURS after Araghchi formally named China as 'friendly' nation. Brent immediately spiked above $111. Iran's selective passage regime is NOT operationally reliable â€” even named-friendly nations can't transit consistently. Possible causes: IRGC Navy C2 void (Tangsiri dead, no replacement), corridor coordination breakdown, or mines/military activity making transit too risky.

TRIPOLI ARG + 31ST MEU TRANSFERRED TO CENTCOM AOR â€” confirmed Friday Mar 27. USS Tripoli, USS New Orleans, ~2,200 Marines from 31st MEU now in CENTCOM's area. Officials: 'several additional days' to reach Strait of Hormuz. Tier 2 indicator (Persian Gulf entry) now on clock â€” watch for AIS going dark or straight run to Gulf vs Gulf of Oman park.

TRUMP CONSIDERING 10,000 MORE GROUND TROOPS to Middle East (WSJ/Axios). On TOP of ~5,000 Marines (2 MEUs) + ~3,000 82nd Airborne already ordered. Would bring total to 15,000-18,000 ground troops. Includes infantry AND armored vehicles. Tehran responds: 'one million fighters ready for confrontation.' This is far beyond Kharg-only operation sizing â€” suggests multiple objective force or sustained occupation capability.
evidenceLog.source: WSJ/Axios/Fox News/JPost/Stars and Stripes/Voice of Emirates

evidenceLog.text: IDF struck Yazd â€” Iran's PRIMARY facility for production of missiles AND SEA MINES. Used for planning, development, assembly, storage of naval missiles (cruise platforms, submarines, helicopters) and sea mines. This directly targets Iran's blockade sustainment capability. Combined with 140+ naval vessels destroyed (~50 mine layers), Iran's ability to RESUPPLY mines is degrading. But ~12 mines already in Hormuz + existing infrastructure persists.

TOMAHAWK DEPLETION CRISIS: 850+ Tomahawks fired in 4 weeks. 319 in first 72 hours alone = 10% of entire US stockpile (~4,000 total). Production: only 90-100/year (RTX planning to scale to 1,000/yr but years away). Pentagon officials 'alarmed.' WaPo, Small Wars Journal, 19FortyFive all reporting. Combined US+Israel: 25,000+ total munitions dropped since Feb 28. Sustainment constraint emerging â€” pushes toward ground ops (fewer precision munitions needed) or de-escalation.

Iran fired NEW missile/drone waves at Gulf states overnight. Sirens in Bahrain, Qatar, UAE. UAE intercepted ballistic missile over Abu Dhabi â€” debris killed 2, injured 3 (UAE total death toll now 11). Saudi intercepted drones over Eastern Province. Kuwait intercepted missiles/drones, loud explosions heard. Iran attacking Gulf states DAILY now â€” sustained regional war.

IDF 'wide wave of attacks overnight in Tehran' â€” targeted military industries, launch sites, soldiers. Also struck Parchin again + western Iran missile launchers/storage. Air raids killed 6 in Qom (three residential houses). Urmia: residential complex hit, 4 buildings destroyed. Katz: strikes will 'intensify and expand.' War Day 28.
evidenceLog.source: Times of Israel liveblog Mar 27/Al Jazeera/Boston Globe/NBC

evidenceLog.text: Brent surged above $111/bbl (+3.2% morning) on Chinese ship U-turn at Hormuz. Brent-WTI spread widened to $12.45 â€” reflects acute premium for waterborne crude. WTI futures $97.01 (+2.68%). S&P 500 futures down 0.4%, on course for FIFTH consecutive weekly decline. 10yr Treasury yield climbed to 4.46%. 30yr mortgage at 6.38%. Bloomberg: 'Iran War Is Now All About the Future of the Strait of Hormuz.'

Iran Red Crescent: 1,900 killed, 20,000 injured (official Iranian figure â€” significantly lower than Hengaw's 6,530). 7 Hormuz transits confirmed Mar 26 (non-Iranian vessels) â€” but Chinese U-turn shows corridor unreliable. 20,000 sailors stranded in Persian Gulf (NBC). Windward maritime intelligence: movement through Hormuz 'no longer halted but remains selective, controlled, and only partially visible.'

Posteriors HELD. Competing dynamics: (1) Chinese ship U-turn = blockade MORE severe than Iran claims, even 'friendly' ships can't reliably transit (H3/H4 up), (2) BUT Yazd sea mine factory destroyed + 140 naval vessels = Iran's SUSTAINMENT capability degrading (H2 up), (3) 10,000 more troops = phase transition from air degradation to ground ops emerging (extends crisis but could resolve Hormuz faster via seizure), (4) Tomahawk depletion = US running out of standoff options, creates pressure for either ground or de-escalation, (5) Tripoli now in CENTCOM AOR. These dynamics roughly offset. War transitioning from Phase 1 (air degradation, diminishing returns at <100 targets/day) to Phase 2 (ground/maritime). That transition will take days-weeks to materialize. Hold â€” Apr 6 deadline + Tripoli position remain key decision points.

MASSIVE ESCALATION: Israel struck TWO Iranian nuclear facilities for FIRST TIME in war. (1) Arak heavy water reactor (Khondab complex) â€” 'key plutonium production site for nuclear weapons.' (2) Ardakan yellowcake production plant (Yazd Province) â€” produces uranium ore feedstock for enrichment centrifuges. Also hit 2 of Iran's largest steel factories + a power plant. Iran confirmed damage but says no casualties or radioactive leaks. Araghchi: strikes 'contradict' Trump's extension. Tehran vows retaliation 'will no longer be an eye for an eye.' This crosses a threshold â€” nuclear facility strikes make diplomatic resolution significantly harder.

Iran ACTIVELY BLOCKED Chinese Cosco ships at Hormuz â€” not just a U-turn. IRGC naval forces turned around three ships total: CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean (both Chinese/Cosco), and Lotus Rising (Marshall Islands flag). FDD confirms. This happened ONE DAY after Araghchi formally named China as 'friendly nation' with transit rights. Iran's selective passage regime is OPERATIONALLY DEAD. Blockade is effectively total regardless of diplomatic announcements. CSIS: 'No one, not even Beijing, is getting through.'

RUBIO AT G7: War will continue '2-4 more weeks.' FIRST official admission war extends beyond initial 4-6 week timeline. Total = 6-8 weeks minimum from Feb 28 (mid-Apr to end-Apr). Also claimed US 'close to holding serious negotiations' while simultaneously considering 10,000 more troops. European G7 members pushing de-escalation. Divisions emerging in Western alliance.

US INTELLIGENCE: Only 1/3 of Iran's missile arsenal CONFIRMED destroyed after a month of war. Another 1/3 damaged or buried underground. Remaining 1/3 intact and operational. Iran can sustain missile attacks for MONTHS. Contradicts White House 'ahead of schedule' narrative. Combined with Tomahawk depletion (850+ fired, ~20% of stockpile), both sides face long-term sustainment challenges but neither is close to exhaustion.

IRGC issued warning to CIVILIANS near US forces in Gulf states to EVACUATE. Statement: 'We are duty-bound to target US forces wherever we find them.' Called US forces 'cowardly' for using 'civilian locations as human shields.' This is pre-attack notification language â€” signals imminent escalation against US bases in Gulf. Combined with nuclear site strikes, Iran's retaliation 'will no longer be eye for eye.'

Brent closed at $112.57 (+4.22%) â€” HIGHEST since July 2022 (Russia/Ukraine). Brent-WTI spread $12.45. Dow tumbled ~800 points, ENTERED CORRECTION TERRITORY. S&P 500 posted FIFTH consecutive weekly decline. OECD: US inflation to hit 4.2% in 2026, global growth cut to 2.9%. UK worst-hit among major economies. Fortune analyst: war 'could drag into fall 2027.'

10,000 troop decision expected NEXT WEEK (per defense official). Not yet approved. Rubio: deployments give Trump 'flexibility if conditions change.' Infantry + armored vehicles = ground operation capability, not just deterrence posture. Meanwhile: Tripoli in CENTCOM AOR, 82nd deploying, Boxer approaching. Force convergence continuing regardless of diplomatic track.

POSTERIORS UPDATED. H2 0.16â†’0.13, H3 0.53â†’0.54, H4 0.30â†’0.32. E[weeks] 39.32â†’40.63. Rationale: (1) Israel struck NUCLEAR FACILITIES = massive escalation that kills near-term diplomatic path. Iran will harden, not negotiate, after Arak/Ardakan. (2) Iran BLOCKED Chinese ships at Hormuz = selective passage regime is dead on arrival. Blockade is effectively total. (3) Only 1/3 missiles destroyed = Iran can sustain for months. (4) Rubio '2-4 more weeks' = first official timeline extension. (5) IRGC evacuation warning = asymmetric retaliation incoming. All ACTIONS not rhetoric. Near-term resolution (H2) probability compressing. Conflict hardening into extended war of attrition.

IRGC EXECUTED EVACUATION WARNING: Iran struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. 12 US service members injured (shrapnel wounds, not life-threatening). Refueler tanker aircraft damaged. This came HOURS after IRGC warned civilians to evacuate areas near US forces. Retaliatory cycle for nuclear site strikes (Arak/Ardakan) playing out. First confirmed US casualties from Iranian strike since Kuwait airport attack. War expanding into direct Iran-vs-US-bases axis.

HOUTHIS ENTER THE WAR: Yemen's Houthis fired barrage of ballistic missiles at 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel. IDF intercepted one missile. Brig Gen Saree announced on Al Masirah TV. FIRST Houthi attack on Israel since Hamas truce. WAR EXPANDED TO NEW FRONT â€” now 5 countries directly attacking (US, Israel, Iran, Hezbollah/Lebanon, Houthis/Yemen). Houthis can threaten Bab al-Mandab Strait = potential SECOND maritime choke point. If Houthis resume Red Sea shipping attacks, Cape route becomes even longer/costlier.

IRGC NAMED 15 US COMPANIES AS TARGETS + 29 LOCATIONS: Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Oracle, Amazon (AWS), Boeing, ExxonMobil, Lockheed Martin, IBM, Palantir. Facilities in Bahrain, Israel, Qatar, UAE. Already attacked 3 AWS datacenters. IRGC expanding from military to ECONOMIC WARFARE â€” targeting US tech/defense/energy infrastructure across Gulf. This is 'infrastructure warfare' per The Register.
evidenceLog.source: Inc/Nation Thailand/Middle East Eye/Middle East Monitor/The Register

evidenceLog.text: Iran struck Tel Aviv overnight â€” 8 impact sites including a university. 1 killed, 2 injured. Kuwait airport radar system damaged by drone. UAE: falling debris from intercepts caused fires at industrial hub. Israel continued striking Tehran, Isfahan, Khuzestan steel plants. 120+ historical sites damaged across Iran. Bushehr NPP struck AGAIN (Iran says no damage/casualties). War at full intensity despite 'talks.'

WTI briefly touched $100.04 â€” first time above $100 since 2022. Closed at $99.64 (+5.46%). Brent $112.57. Goldman Sachs: $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium baked in. Hormuz transits down to 5/day (from 120 pre-war) per Windward. 101 eastbound transits vs 41 westbound = ships fleeing the Gulf, not entering. OECD: US inflation to hit 4.2%, global growth 2.9%.

CNN: Trump's conflicting signals 'frustrate GOP lawmakers and allies' â€” no clear endgame after 4 weeks. House Armed Services Chairman Rogers: 'frustration on both sides of the aisle.' GOP Rep Mace stormed out of briefing. Only 20% of Republicans support ground troops. Congress may block additional war funding without clarity. Trump simultaneously claims talks 'going well' while ordering thousands more troops. Domestic political constraint on escalation tightening.

ONE MONTH MARK: WaPo: ~1,500 Iranian civilians killed (most comprehensive estimate). NRC: 1M+ displaced in Iran. 81,000+ civilian structures damaged (61,000 homes, 19,000 commercial, 275 medical centers, ~500 schools). US: 13 KIA, 200+ wounded across 7 countries, 12 injured at Prince Sultan Air Base Mar 27. Israel: 19 civilians killed, 5,229+ injured. Lebanon: 1,072+ killed. Gulf states: 25+ killed (UAE total 11+).

Posteriors HELD this cycle. Houthi entry is the headline but ONE salvo doesn't confirm sustained campaign. Key question: do Houthis resume RED SEA SHIPPING attacks? If yes, Bab al-Mandab becomes second choke point = catastrophic for global trade and extends Hormuz resolution timeline (ships can't even route around). IRGC economic warfare (15 US companies targeted) + Prince Sultan strike = war broadening into new domains. But yesterday's move already captured the nuclear escalation + Chinese ship blockage dynamics. Wait for: (1) Houthi follow-on attacks, (2) Bab al-Mandab shipping threat, (3) 10,000 troop decision next week, (4) Tripoli position update.

Trump's third deadline (Apr 6 8pm ET) expired. Iran rejected 45-day ceasefire proposal. Trump extended 24hrs to Apr 7 8pm ET, declared it 'FINAL.' Third blink confirmed. Trump warned 'whole civilization will die' if no deal.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Pattern continuation — third deadline extension. ultimatum_expire already FIRED for this pattern.
evidenceLog.note: extensionCount now 3

evidenceLog.text: US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island. 38+ explosions reported across the island. Oil infrastructure deliberately avoided. Military sites comprehensively hit. Strikes occurred same day as ceasefire agreement.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: bushehr_strikes FIRED + new_phase FIRED. Applied pre-committed effects: H3/H4 up, coastal prep confirmed.
evidenceLog.note: Kharg military sites — NOT oil infrastructure

evidenceLog.text: USS Tripoli positioned offshore Kharg Island. F-35Bs conducting SEAD missions suppressing S-300 batteries on mainland. Navy SEALs and Marine Raiders began 'Quiet Entry' ops on southern tip of island, cutting undersea fiber-optic cables linking Kharg sensors to mainland C2.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: persian_gulf_entry FIRED. Kharg +10-15pp applied. Tripoli confirmed in Persian Gulf at Kharg, not parked in Gulf of Oman.
evidenceLog.note: Some sourcing from less-established outlets. SEAL/Raider ops details unconfirmed by Pentagon.

evidenceLog.text: IRGC claimed missile strike hit USS Tripoli, forcing vessel to withdraw. Carried by Iranian state media. No confirmation or response from US officials regarding ship condition.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Unconfirmed IRGC claim. If confirmed, would suggest Kharg assault facing resistance but also confirms Tripoli was in strike range of Kharg.
evidenceLog.note: Iranian state media only — trust 0.40. IRGC has history of exaggerated claims.

evidenceLog.text: Pakistan brokered 2-week ceasefire between US and Iran. Trump suspended bombing. Iran agreed to allow 'safe passage through Strait of Hormuz via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.' First face-to-face US-Iran engagement framework since 2015 JCPOA. Ceasefire terms: halt hostilities, reopen Hormuz, 15-20 day negotiation period.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: ceasefire anti-indicator FIRED. Applied pre-committed effect: H1/H2 surge (conservative — H1 0.01→0.03, H2 0.13→0.22). Ceasefire fragile from hour 1.

evidenceLog.text: Israel launched massive strikes on Lebanon/Hezbollah on Day 1 of ceasefire. 254 killed in single day. Netanyahu declared 'Lebanon not included in ceasefire.' Trump agreed — said Lebanon excluded. Pakistan (mediator) said Lebanon WAS included. Iran accused US of 'grave violation.'
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Ceasefire violation weakens H1/H2 surge but does not reverse it alone. Lebanon dispute is separate from Hormuz mechanism.
evidenceLog.note: Lebanon exclusion dispute immediately undermined ceasefire credibility

evidenceLog.text: Hormuz NOT reopened despite ceasefire agreement. Ships still prevented from passing through strait on Day 1 of ceasefire. Iran promised safe passage but did not implement it. No sign of blockade lifting.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Weakens ceasefire anti-indicator effect — Iran agreed to reopen but didn't follow through. Operational blockade unchanged.

evidenceLog.text: Hormuz traffic crept up to ~10 transits/day during ceasefire period (vs 5/day pre-ceasefire, 120+ pre-war). Still 92% below normal. 600+ vessels (325 tankers) remain stranded in the Gulf.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Marginal improvement insufficient to meet resolution criterion (40 transits/day). 600 stranded vessels = massive backlog even if strait fully reopens.

evidenceLog.text: Brent crude fell from $112.57 (Mar 27) to ~$97.78 on ceasefire hopes. WTI tracking similarly. Market pricing partial reopening but not full resolution.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Market movement on sentiment, not structural change. Blockade still operational.

evidenceLog.text: Iranian delegation arrived in Islamabad for face-to-face talks with US. Pakistani PM Sharif hosting. Israel-Lebanon ceasefire talks announced separately, described as 'historic.'
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Diplomatic process in motion but outcome unknown. Talks track being tested.

evidenceLog.text: Vance, Witkoff, and Kushner met Iranian officials in Islamabad. 21-hour marathon talks. TALKS COLLAPSED. Iran refused US red lines: end all uranium enrichment, fully open Hormuz without tolls. Iran demanded: frozen $6B assets, nuclear program guarantees, right to charge Hormuz tolls, end Israeli attacks on Lebanon. First face-to-face since 2015.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: Reverses ceasefire anti-indicator effect. Diplomatic track exhausted for near term. H2 collapses back, H3/H4 resurge.
evidenceLog.note: Sticking points irreconcilable in near term — nuclear enrichment + Hormuz tolls are existential for both sides

evidenceLog.text: USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy transited Strait of Hormuz for mine-clearing operations. CENTCOM confirmed. UUV drones (torpedo-shaped, high-res sonar) deployed to scan seabed for Maham 3 (moored) and Maham 7 (bottom) mines. Additional forces to 'join clearance effort in coming days.' UK mobilizing coalition with France for minesweeping support.
evidenceLog.source: CENTCOM press release/CBS/Al Jazeera/Gulf News/DefenseScoop/Task & Purpose
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: new_phase FIRED (already counted). Mine clearing = physical prerequisite for reopening. Weeks-to-months timeline for clearing. Confirms Hormuz physically blocked, not just politically.
evidenceLog.note: Mine clearing is slow work — typically weeks to certify lanes safe for commercial shipping

evidenceLog.text: Three oil supertankers (2 Chinese, 1 Greek) transited Strait of Hormuz — biggest day of oil shipping since war began. Occurred during ceasefire window.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Proof of concept that transit is physically possible, but 3 ships vs 120+/day pre-war. Ceasefire window only.

evidenceLog.text: Trump announced via Truth Social: 'Effective immediately, the United States Navy will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz.' Will intercept vessels that paid Iran's toll. Will destroy Iranian mines. STRAIT NOW UNDER DUAL BLOCKADE — Iran blocking from east, US blocking from west.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: Massive H3/H4 signal. Dual blockade unprecedented — neither side permitting free transit. Resolution criterion (40 transits/day) now requires BOTH sides to stand down. Dramatically extends expected closure duration.
evidenceLog.note: US counter-blockade transforms crisis from Iranian closure to mutual closure

evidenceLog.text: IRGC vowed to retaliate against any military vessels entering the strait. Iran warns mine-clearing ships will be targeted. UK said it is building 'wide coalition' with France and others to ensure passage.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. RHETORIC — threat of escalation but no kinetic action yet against mine-clearing ships.

evidenceLog.text: Houthis limited involvement to strikes on Israel. Have NOT resumed Red Sea shipping attacks despite threats. Analysts assess strategic restraint under regional pressure. Bab al-Mandeb traffic at ~50% of normal from earlier disruption but no new attacks on commercial shipping.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Anti-H4 signal — second choke point NOT activated. Alternative Cape route remains viable. Reduces worst-case dual-chokepoint scenario.
evidenceLog.note: Key watch: if Houthis start hitting Red Sea shipping, H4 surges

evidenceLog.text: Iran reportedly unable to locate all naval mines it planted in Strait of Hormuz. Sea currents may have swept some mines from original positions, complicating both Iranian and US clearance efforts.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional (lost mines extend closure timeline) but no indicator fired. Mine clearance complexity is not a pre-registered indicator threshold.
evidenceLog.note: Source trust: Euronews baseTrust 0.75. claimState PROPOSED because single-source, not independently confirmed. Lost mines increase hazard duration regardless of diplomatic outcome.

evidenceLog.text: IRGC Navy Command stated passage through Strait of Hormuz would only be granted to civilian vessels under specific conditions. Military vessels explicitly excluded. Broadcast via Iranian state media (IRIB).
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Consistent with existing IRGC posture in ev_123. No indicator fired. Refines the access regime but does not represent a new escalation or de-escalation threshold.
evidenceLog.note: Source trust: Iranian state media baseTrust 0.50 but this is an official IRGC statement about its own policy, not a factual claim about external events. Weight 0.8 reflects direct-source reliability for self-reported policy positions.

evidenceLog.text: US-Iran ceasefire collapsed after 3 days. Two US warships sailed through Strait of Hormuz to establish merchant shipping corridor. Transits remain at less than 10% of pre-war daily flow (~40 transits/day baseline). Iran continues requiring ships to obtain permission to transit.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional. Ceasefire collapse reverses brief de-escalation signal. Traffic at <10% confirms continued effective closure. No new indicator threshold met beyond existing fired indicators.
evidenceLog.note: Ceasefire lasted only ~72 hours. Iranian control over strait transit never actually lifted.

evidenceLog.text: Islamabad peace talks between US and Iran ended without agreement. Trump ordered immediate US naval blockade of all Iranian ports. CENTCOM began implementing blockade April 13 at 10am ET. Dual blockade: Iran controls Hormuz transit while US blocks Iranian port access.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H4-directional. Dual blockade adds closure complexity but no specific indicator defines US counter-blockade as firing threshold. Existing escalation indicators already fired. Posteriors already reflect extended closure at H3=0.51/H4=0.38.
evidenceLog.note: Dual blockade unprecedented in modern naval history. Makes diplomatic resolution harder. Monitor for new indicator definitions.

evidenceLog.text: CENTCOM reports first 24 hours of US naval blockade: 6 merchant ships turned around and returned to Iranian ports after encountering blockade. No ships made it past. 10,000+ US service members and 12 Navy ships enforcing blockade 'against vessels of all nations' coming in or out of Iranian ports. US clarified blockade targets Iranian port traffic only — not full strait closure. Ships transiting without entering/exiting Iranian ports told it is safe to pass. US sent destroyers through Hormuz to demonstrate safe corridor.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Confirms existing blockade posture from ev_122/ev_128. No new indicator fired. US clarification that strait itself is not fully blocked is marginally H2-directional (less severe than full dual closure) but offset by continued Iranian mine presence + Iranian transit controls. Operational data consistent with H3/H4 distribution.
evidenceLog.note: CNN EVENT domainTrust 0.99. CENTCOM primary source. Key nuance: US blockade is Iranian-port-specific, not full strait closure. Iran's mine barrier + transit permission regime still the binding constraint on reopening.

evidenceLog.text: No agreement on second round of US-Iran talks. US officials exploring dates/locations before ceasefire expires April 21. Trump suggests Pakistan round 2 ('something could be happening in next 2 days'), dismisses Geneva. Iran state media (IRNA): no decision on another round. Both sides proposed uranium enrichment suspension but disagree on timespan. US demands dismantling of enrichment facilities + unconditional Hormuz reopening. Separately, France/UK announced Friday video conference to build coalition for defensive freedom-of-navigation mission. Macron urged Hormuz reopening 'without controls or tolls.' 18 European + Australian foreign ministers issued joint statement calling for Lebanon ceasefire and regional de-escalation. Israel-Lebanon highest-level direct talks in 40+ years began in DC; Lebanon seeks ceasefire as precondition.
evidenceLog.source: CNN (citing IRNA, Macron/X, UK gov statement, multiple officials)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Diplomatic track weak but not dead — ceasefire expiry April 21 creates pressure. No talks-related indicator fires. FON coalition is announcement-only, no operational capability yet. Israel-Lebanon talks relevant because Iran demanded Lebanon ceasefire inclusion as precondition. Consistent with talksTrack maximalist_deadlock (0.55).
evidenceLog.note: CNN DIPLO domainTrust 0.2463 (historically poor on diplomatic predictions). PROPOSED claimState for talks speculation. FON coalition formation is new diplomatic thread — monitor for operational commitment. effectiveWeight attenuated by CNN DIPLO track record.

evidenceLog.text: IMF chief economist says any Hormuz tolls likely passed to consumers. At least one vessel paid $2M toll to transit (Lloyd's List). Capital Economics: toll burden 'shared between producers and consumers' but 'overall impact on global prices and demand would probably be limited.' US gas prices $4.12/gallon (AAA, Apr 14), down 5 cents from $4.17 peak post-ceasefire. Oil futures slightly lower in early Tuesday trading. Experts don't expect gas below $4 for 1-2 weeks, could be months before sub-$3 (pre-war level).
evidenceLog.source: CNN (citing IMF, Lloyd's List, Capital Economics, AAA)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. ECON data on toll pricing and gas trajectory. No indicator threshold met. Gas price decline is marginal and may reverse with blockade. Toll system being priced by markets as semi-permanent — H3/H4-directional (tolls assume prolonged Iranian control). Capital Economics 'limited impact' assessment applies to tolls specifically, not the broader closure.
evidenceLog.note: CNN effectiveTrust 0.7462 (ECON domain not calibrated, using cross-topic overall). Multiple primary sources cited (IMF, Lloyd's List, AAA). Gas data updates stale dataFeed.

evidenceLog.text: Pentagon confirmed zero ships made it past US blockade in first 24 hours of enforcement. Six merchant ships followed orders to turn around. Blockade is operational and 100% effective at stopping Iranian port traffic. China called the blockade a "dangerous and irresponsible act" that will further inflame tensions. Iran accused the US of "piracy" and stated ports are "either for everyone or for no one." Separately, CNN reports satellite imagery shows Iran using ceasefire period to clear debris from entrances to underground missile bases.
evidenceLog.source: Al Jazeera (Pentagon) / CNBC (China MFA) / CNN (satellite imagery)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional — 100% blockade effectiveness on day 1 confirms dual closure is operational. China diplomatic condemnation adds international friction to resolution pathway. Iran debris-clearing suggests preparation for post-ceasefire military posture, not de-escalation. However no unfired indicator threshold is met. Remaining indicators are Kharg-operation-specific (NOTAM, AIS dark, Trump silence, evac orders) and diplomatic de-escalation triggers (escort announced, ARG split). Posteriors remain H3=0.51/H4=0.38.
evidenceLog.note: Day 44 of closure. Blockade enforcement data is the first hard metric on US operational effectiveness. China MFA statement is the strongest diplomatic response from a non-belligerent — could pressure UN Security Council action. Iran missile base activity is an escalation signal under ceasefire cover.

evidenceLog.text: Iran army warned it will block trade through the Red Sea, Gulf of Oman, and Sea of Oman if the US naval blockade on Iranian ports continues — an explicit threat to expand closure beyond Hormuz. Separately, Trump stated the war is "very close to over" and said Islamabad round 2 is "more likely." US military confirmed it has "completely halted economic trade going into and out of Iran by sea" on day 2 of blockade. 15 US ships deployed including aircraft carrier and 11 destroyers.
evidenceLog.source: Al Jazeera (Iran army threat) / ABC News (Trump quotes) / CNN (force posture)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Iran Red Sea threat is H4-directional (wider closure = longer duration to resolve). Trump "close to over" is H2-directional rhetoric but RHETORIC tag attenuates weight — no observable threshold met. Force posture data (15 ships) confirms sustained commitment. No unfired indicator matches: Red Sea expansion threat is a novel escalation vector with no pre-registered indicator. Consider adding indicator for Iranian geographic escalation beyond Hormuz.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45 of closure. Iran Red Sea threat is significant — could cross-impact calibration-houthi-posture topic if executed. Trump optimistic rhetoric contradicts blockade escalation; watch for action vs words divergence. Ceasefire expires April 21 — 6 days remain.

evidenceLog.text: CENTCOM confirmed US blockade of Iranian ports 'fully implemented' as of Tuesday night. In first 36 hours: 10+ merchant vessels turned back, 20+ non-Iranian ships transited safely. US destroyer interdicted two oil tankers leaving Iran's Chabahar port (Gulf of Oman — extends blockade beyond Hormuz to non-Gulf ports). Force posture: 10,000+ service members, 12+ ships, 100+ fighter/surveillance aircraft. CENTCOM broadcast radio warning: vessels attempting to breach blockade 'will be boarded for interdiction and seizure.' However, tracking data shows at least 3 Iran-linked vessels (Christianna, Elpis, others) crossed strait after blockade began — blockade not 100% effective.
evidenceLog.source: CBS News (CENTCOM press release) / Al Jazeera (Pentagon) / Kpler (tracking data)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional — blockade operationally enforced at scale, Chabahar interdiction extends closure beyond Hormuz. However, 3 Iran-linked ships still crossing shows gaps. Updates ev_129/ev_132 with Day 2 data but no unfired indicator threshold met. Existing indicators already cover blockade posture.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45. Chabahar interdiction is new — demonstrates US will enforce beyond Hormuz proper. CBS EVENT domainTrust high. Kpler DATA tracking reliable. Partially recycled from ev_129/ev_132 but Chabahar + force numbers are new data.

evidenceLog.text: Bloomberg reports US and Iran have 'in principle agreement' to pursue further diplomacy after inconclusive Islamabad round 1. Pakistan PM Sharif traveling to Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Turkey this week to arrange second round. Ceasefire extension being considered before April 21 expiry. Trump told Fox: 'I think it's close to over, yeah.' Trump told NY Post: talks could happen 'over the next two days.' Mediators pushing for compromise on Hormuz and nuclear program before truce expires.
evidenceLog.source: Bloomberg / CBS News (Trump quotes) / Fox News
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H2-directional rhetoric — 'in principle agreement' is weaker than actual talks date. Trump 'close to over' is rhetoric without observable basis (blockade continues, no Hormuz reopening, mine barrier intact). Ceasefire extension consideration is H2-directional but no indicator fires. Anti-indicator 'ceasefire' already FIRED and effect reversed. No new anti-indicator threshold met.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45. Bloomberg DIPLO trust unrated — using cross-topic overall. Trump rhetoric discount applies per actorModel methodology. 'In principle agreement' is not a date or venue — weight heavily attenuated. Sharif shuttle diplomacy is concrete action (LOG) but effect uncertain.

evidenceLog.text: US official told Axios: Washington sees 'no need at present' to launch military invasion of Kharg Island. Blockade strategy substituting for ground seizure — official stated 'the ability to strangle the island is available without direct military escalation.' Evaluation includes increased naval presence, enhanced surveillance, coordinated allied pressure, and economic restrictions as alternatives to amphibious assault.
evidenceLog.source: Axios (citing US official) / Voice of Emirates
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Potentially H2/H3-directional — if blockade replaces ground seizure, closure duration may be shorter (blockade can be lifted faster than territorial seizure reversed). However, anti-indicator 'trump_denies_kharg' requires TRUMP personally saying 'we don't need Kharg,' not a unnamed official. Indicator NOT FIRED. Does not meet 'escort_announced' either — blockade enforcement ≠ escort ops. meuMission.kharg probability should be monitored for reassessment but no posterior change warranted without fired indicator.
evidenceLog.note: Axios EVENT domainTrust 0.99, but this is INTEL/POLICY domain. Using cross-topic overall 0.99. PROPOSED because single anonymous official — not confirmed Pentagon position. If Trump personally echoes this, anti-indicator trump_denies_kharg would fire.

evidenceLog.text: Israel and Lebanon held first direct talks since 1993 in Washington with Secretary Rubio participating. Joint statement: agreed to 'launch direct negotiations.' Simultaneously: first Israeli soldier killed in combat since ceasefire (Bint Jbeil), IDF fully surrounded Bint Jbeil, Hezbollah targeted 13 northern Israeli towns with rockets during talks. Talks and fighting occurring in parallel.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. Indirectly H2-directional — Iran demanded Israel-Lebanon ceasefire as precondition in Islamabad talks. Progress on this track could remove one sticking point. But simultaneous combat (IDF KIA, Hezbollah rockets) shows talks ≠ ceasefire. No Hormuz-specific indicator fires. Cross-topic dependency with calibration-houthi-posture noted.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45. Dual signal: diplomatic progress (first direct talks in 33 years) and kinetic escalation (first IDF combat KIA since ceasefire). Net effect ambiguous for Hormuz — depends whether Lebanon track feeds back into US-Iran negotiations.

evidenceLog.text: IMF sharply cut 2026 MENA growth forecast to 1.1% (from prior estimate). Iran economy predicted to slide 6.1%. Iraq and Qatar also particularly hard-hit. EIA raised 2026 Brent crude projection to $96/bbl. Brent trading $94.66-96.80 on Apr 15, volatile but range-bound around $95. 103 empty oil tankers heading to US ports to load crude. US gas $4.12/gal (AAA Apr 14), down 5 cents from $4.17 peak.
evidenceLog.source: IMF / EIA / CBS News (citing AAA, tanker tracking)
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional — IMF forecast pricing in prolonged disruption, EIA $96 Brent projection assumes sustained closure. Iran -6.1% GDP may increase domestic pressure to negotiate OR harden regime survival posture. No indicator threshold met. Brent range-bound near $95 — no defense_stocks spike on no news.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45. IMF/EIA are institutional primary sources — high trust. 103 tankers to US ports suggests US positioning as alternative supplier, reducing global pressure to reopen Hormuz quickly. Updates stale dataFeeds.

evidenceLog.text: AP reports US and Iran have "in principle agreement" to extend the two-week ceasefire (expiring April 21) to allow more diplomacy. However a senior US official told CNBC the US "has not formally agreed" to an extension. Pakistani army chief is in Tehran pushing to restart talks. White House says it feels "good about prospects of a deal" and Pakistan is likely location for round 2. Trump: war is "very close to over," "we have beaten them militarily, totally."
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H2-directional (ceasefire extension + resumed talks could shorten closure). But ceasefire indicator already fired and the "in principle" report is contradicted by US official denial — claimState PROPOSED, not SUPPORTED. Enrichment gap (20yr vs 5yr) remains unresolved. No indicator fires. Weight 0.7: DIPLO tag but mixed signals reduce confidence.
evidenceLog.note: Ceasefire extension is the key signal. If formally confirmed, watch for whether Hormuz reopening is a precondition. The Apr 8 ceasefire was supposed to include reopening but Iran never actually restored traffic.

evidenceLog.text: France and UK announced online meeting April 17 to assemble a "defensive multilateral mission" for keeping the Strait of Hormuz open. Macron urged reopening "without controls or tolls." Starmer and Trump discussed both diplomatic coalition and military options on April 9 call. 18 European + Australian foreign ministers issued joint statement calling for regional de-escalation.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H2-directional (multilateral naval mission could restore partial traffic independent of Iran-US deal). Anti-indicator escort_announced requires "Pentagon announces escort ops with MEU aviation" — this is a Franco-British initiative, not Pentagon/MEU. Close but not a match. April 17 meeting is the next observable: if coalition forms and deploys escorts, revisit.
evidenceLog.note: This is a third pathway to reopening (alongside Iran-US deal and unilateral US mine-clearing). European-led escort missions have precedent (Operation Sentinel, 2019). Watch April 17 meeting outcome.

evidenceLog.text: 3,000+ vessels backed up in the Gulf area including 800+ oil tankers (Reuters). Maritime officials say it will take months to restore normal traffic even after the strait opens. Iran previously granted selective transit to China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, and Thailand on March 26 — a sovereignty-based access regime that bypasses the US blockade for friendly states. Enrichment sticking point: US demands 20-year halt, Iran proposes 5 years.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional — "months to restore" even post-reopening is a strong signal that effective closure extends well beyond any political agreement. 3,000 vessel backlog is the largest maritime backup in modern history. Iran selective access (7 nations) creates a two-tier system that complicates resolution criteria ("sustained >30% of pre-war traffic"). No indicator fires.
evidenceLog.note: Resolution criteria is >30% of pre-war traffic (~40 transits/day). The "months to restore" data point suggests that even if political resolution occurs, operational resolution lags significantly. This favors H3 over H2.

evidenceLog.text: CENTCOM declared US naval blockade of Iranian ports 'fully implemented' within 36 hours of Trump's order. 10,000+ US personnel involved. 6 merchant ships turned around in first 24 hours. Interdictions in Gulf of Oman, not Hormuz proper. However, 19 vessels still transited Hormuz on Apr 15 (5 inbound, 14 outbound, various flags) and sanctioned ships crossed toward Iran despite CENTCOM's 'completely halted' claim. Blockade covers Iranian ports in Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman.
evidenceLog.source: CENTCOM press release / Al Jazeera / CBS News / CNBC / CNN
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional — full blockade deepens dual-blockade deadlock, increases resolution prerequisites. 19 transits on Apr 15 (vs 10/day ceasefire avg) shows blockade porous. No indicator threshold met. escort_announced requires Pentagon MEU escort ops — blockade is offensive interdiction, not escort.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45. CENTCOM CMDR Cooper announced. Tension: CENTCOM says completely halted but 19 ships transited same day. Updates hormuz_traffic to 19 transits.

evidenceLog.text: Mohsen Rezaei (Khamenei military adviser, ex-IRGC CinC) threatened to sink US ships in Hormuz: 'These ships will be sunk by our first missiles.' Separately, Iran armed forces warned they would block shipping in Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea if US blockade continues. Red Sea threat is new — first explicit Iranian (not Houthi) threat to Bab al-Mandab chokepoint.
evidenceLog.source: Manila Times / NBC News / CNN / Fortune
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H4-directional — Red Sea expansion threat triggers Houthi swing factor watchpoint if executed. RHETORIC not ACTION. rhetoric_as_evidence lint: Rezaei threat is political signaling. IRGC has not attacked US ships despite weeks of proximity. Weight 0.5: RHETORIC tag, unexecuted threat. TTL=24h.
evidenceLog.note: Day 45. RED SEA extension is critical new signal. Previous threats targeted Hormuz only. Suggests Iran may coordinate Houthis if blockade tightens. Cross-topic: calibration-houthi-posture.

evidenceLog.text: France and UK announced April 17 online meeting to assemble defensive multilateral naval mission for Hormuz reopening. Macron urged reopening without controls or tolls.
GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: stale_evidence, quorum_failure. Reason: Franco-British multilateral escort initiative. H2-directional.

AP reports Iran and US gave 'in principle' approval to extend ceasefire beyond Apr 22 expiry, via Pakistan mediation (finance minister Aurangzeb). However, US official told Reuters Washington has 'not formally agreed' to extension. Three sticking points remain: nuclear program, Hormuz control, war damages. Iran demands blockade lifted as precondition. Trump: 'I view it as very close to over.' Ceasefire technically holding with no confirmed violations but structurally precarious with blockade ongoing.
evidenceLog.source: AP (Magdy/Metz/Ahmed) / Reuters / Fortune / Middle East Monitor
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H2-directional: extension creates diplomatic window favoring 6wk-4mo resolution. But 'in principle' is not formal agreement, and three core sticking points (nuclear, Hormuz, damages) are the same ones that collapsed Islamabad talks. No indicator fired.
evidenceLog.note: Day 46. Ceasefire expires Apr 22 (6 days). Gap between in-principle and formal is significant. US may use ambiguity as leverage while blockade tightens. Cross-ref ev_139. talksTrack: supports direct_talks_resume (0.30).

evidenceLog.text: Treasury waiver authorizing certain Iranian crude purchases expires this weekend and will NOT be renewed. Tightens economic blockade beyond naval interdiction. 230 loaded oil tankers remain trapped in Gulf. ECB and IMF issuing stagflation warnings tied to supply disruptions. North Sea oil at record highs.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional: waiver expiry removes last legal channel for Iranian oil exports, deepening economic isolation. Adds resolution prerequisite (sanctions policy reversal). No indicator fired.
evidenceLog.note: Day 46. Source trust 0.50 (unknown in source_db). Waiver expiry verifiable via Treasury.gov. 230 loaded tankers refines stranded_vessels datafeed.

evidenceLog.text: USS Spruance (DDG-111) redirected Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to evade US blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas. Vessel heading back to Iran. Blockade enforcement actively operational with physical interdictions in Gulf of Oman.
evidenceLog.note: Matches watchpoint: US naval blockade implementation - does Navy actually intercept ships?. Domain keywords: blockade, iran, iranian. No pre-registered indicator match — review whether this warrants a new indicator or evidence log entry. Source trust: 50% (moderate).

evidenceLog.text: GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: stale_evidence. Reason: Evidence ev_144: USS Spruance (DDG-111) redirected Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to eva

Pakistani army chief Gen Asim Munir arrived Tehran Apr 15 to push for round 2 US-Iran talks before ceasefire expires Apr 22. Three sticking points from Islamabad round 1: Iran nuclear enrichment (US demands 20-year moratorium), Strait of Hormuz reopening terms, and war damage compensation. Vance expected to lead US delegation for round 2. Framework deal being discussed per Axios.
evidenceLog.note: Matches indicator ceasefire (anti indicators). (already fired). Source trust: 50% (moderate).
